name: Taxonomy Mapping review for Telomerase Restoration bundle
summary: The staged repair maps adjacent telomerase records conservatively. Direct TA-65 biomarker evidence stays in Telomerase Restoration; NCT02530255 is correctly represented as cycloastragenol Alzheimer disease boundary context rather than TA-65 evidence; disease-context Libella AAV-hTERT registries remain gene-therapy boundary/watchlist records; and TERT mRNA radiation-injury biology is kept as a non-aging boundary that also touches DNA repair biology.

findings.claim_or_issue: NCT02530255 is represented as cycloastragenol retinal-amyloid disease-boundary context, not as a TA-65 trial.
findings.why_it_matters: The prior watchlist named this record near TA-65, but the registry intervention is cycloastragenol; conflating them would weaken source fidelity and taxonomy clarity.
findings.recommended_action: No revision needed; keep the cycloastragenol-not-TA-65 caveat.

findings.claim_or_issue: Critical limb ischemia and Alzheimer disease AAV-hTERT registries are mapped as disease-context gene-therapy boundary records with no posted results.
findings.why_it_matters: They are relevant to human AAV-hTERT safety/watchlist coverage but cannot support a broad aging-directed gene-therapy claim.
findings.recommended_action: No revision needed; continue to separate disease-boundary registries from aging-efficacy evidence.

findings.claim_or_issue: TERT mRNA is linked to Telomerase Restoration and DNA Repair Enhancement as boundary biology, with the outlook emphasizing radiation injury and no telomere-length extension.
findings.why_it_matters: The record crosses hallmark biology, but it should not be reclassified as direct telomere-restoration efficacy or broad DNA repair enhancement evidence.

findings.claim_or_issue: The coverage assessment leaves telomere-length-only lifestyle, vitamin, and observational records outside the intervention-focused Telomerase Restoration map.
findings.why_it_matters: That keeps the track scoped to telomerase activation/restoration interventions rather than all telomere-length associations.
findings.recommended_action: No revision needed; revisit only in a future taxonomy pass if observational telomerase context becomes material.
